last_item: Band C: ConfirmDialog → shadcn AlertDialog. Rebuilt ConfirmDialog + TypedConfirmDialog (src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx) on shadcn AlertDialog instead of Modal + hand-rolled .btn. AlertDialog is the right primitive for confirmations (no click-outside dismiss, focus trap, forced explicit choice). Both controlled via open (parent owns close); action/cancel handlers preventDefault() to stop Radix auto-close and keep that contract; Escape ignored while busy (matches old no-op onClose). Buttons → AlertDialogAction/AlertDialogCancel (shadcn Button); danger→variant=destructive + ring-destructive/30. TypedConfirmDialog input → shadcn Field+FieldLabel+Input, keeping the aria-invalid gate + disabled-until-exact-match. Message via AlertDialogDescription asChild over a <div> so callsite block content (Pooler ul/p) stays valid. Added aria-busy={busy}. Public API unchanged — all 6 callsites untouched. Test migrated .btn-danger→data-variant=destructive; destructive-callout query scoped to [data-slot=alert] (action btn now also carries that variant). Deleted dead .confirm-message CSS.
last_result: green: typecheck, 95 web tests, build, go build (outside sandbox; snap-confine cap_dac_override blocks go in-sandbox). ui-heuristics-reviewer: accepted aria-busy status add; rejected (1) disabled→aria-disabled focus-trap churn — conflicts with the locked 'disables both buttons while busy' test invariant + Radix FocusScope already guards focus; (2) Pooler error-announce prop + (3) extra typed-error line — pre-existing / out-of-scope (Pooler is band D; input already label-associated). Same benign dev-only Radix ref warning from the generated alert-dialog.tsx (not a failure). Next in band C: Toast→sonner. Deferred to band E: --info-soft collides with --primary-soft (logged in backlog).
notes: PURE UI/UX LOOP. The prior 57-iteration hardening track is COMPLETE and shipped as v0.6.0. This track rebuilds the web panel on shadcn/ui — see PROMPT.md + UI-RULES.md + backlog.md. The operator EXPLICITLY authorized the shadcn/Tailwind/Radix dependency tree, overriding the earlier rule-4/5 objection — do NOT re-litigate whether to adopt shadcn; it is required. Start at band A (scaffold), then B (shell), C (primitives), D (views one at a time), E (cleanup). Never touch Go/business logic — frontend only.
north_star: Every view in the indiepg panel rebuilt with shadcn/ui — clean, simple, consistent — with no hand-rolled component where a shadcn one exists, behavior identical, all tests green.
